question: The three-state Potts model on a three-dimensional simple cubic lattice is defined by H = -J1 * sum_{nn} delta(sigma_i, sigma_j) - J2 * sum_{nnn} delta(sigma_i, sigma_j), where lambda = J2/J1 is the ratio of next-to-nearest-neighbor to nearest-neighbor coupling. Prior smaller-lattice studies claimed a second-order (Z3 critical) transition for antiferromagnetic lambda=-0.2. Large-scale Monte Carlo simulations on 64^3 lattices with histogram analysis of the nearest-neighbor correlation C_nn and finite-volume peak-width scaling are used to characterize the transition at lambda=-0.2. What is the ORDER of the order-disorder transition of this 3D three-state Potts model at lambda=-0.2? Answer in JSON with field "transition_order" using one of: "first_order", "continuous", "bkt".

metadata.system: 3D three-state Potts model on simple cubic lattice with antiferromagnetic NNN coupling lambda=-0.2
metadata.source_excerpt: For the three-state Potts model on the three-dimensional simple cubic lattice... for the antiferromagnetic ratios lambda=-0.2 and lambda=-0.1, the order-disorder transition is a first-order phase transition. This determination is based on (a) long persistent tunneling between coexisting phases, (b) double-peaked histograms of C_nn, (c) peak widths narrowing as V^{-1/2}
metadata.difficulty_note: Prior work incorrectly claimed second-order Z3 criticality; large-volume simulation on 64^3 reveals weak but robust first-order with clear phase coexistence.

cold_solve.probe_model: claude-sonnet baseline (a cloud HPC platform sandbox for C1)
